title: Improve Performance by Reducing Method Complexity
description: Reducing the cyclomatic complexity of methods can improve performance and code maintainability. This rule is designed to identify complex methods that may need to be simplified or refactored. High complexity in 'equals' and 'hashCode' methods is ignored by this rule.
recommendation: Refactor the complex method to reduce its cyclomatic complexity below the defined threshold. Consider splitting up the method into smaller, more manageable methods.
impact: High cyclomatic complexity can lead to poor performance and difficult-to-maintain code. Addressing this issue can result in more efficient, readable, and maintainable code.

references.title: Methods should not be too complex

checks.message: Refactor the complex method to reduce its cyclomatic complexity below the defined threshold. Consider splitting up the method into smaller, more manageable methods.
